name: Type System Checking
description: A type checker proves a program respects its type discipline: it traverses the syntax tree under a typing environment, inferring or looking up types and applying typing rules to each construct; type variables are unified, incompatibilities raise type errors, and a program with no errors is certified well-typed.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Program AST"]
    N2["Build type environment"]
    N3["Traverse expression"]
    N4["Infer / look up types"]
    N5["Apply typing rules"]
    N6{"Types compatible?"}
    N7["Report type error"]
    N8["Unify type variables"]
    N9{"Unification succeeds?"}
    N10{"More expressions?"}
    N11["Next subexpression"]
    N12["Well-typed program"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 -->|reject| N12
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|no| N7
    N9 -->|yes| N10
    N10 -->|yes| N11
    N11 -->|loop| N3
    N10 -->|no| N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: A theory of type polymorphism in programming
sources.journal: Journal of Computer and System Sciences

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.
